title: Rocket Lab Completes Mynaric Acquisition, Adding Laser Optical Communications To Growing Space Systems Portfolio
text: Rocket Lab Corporation (Nasdaq: RKLB), a global leader in launch services and space systems, announced completion of its acquisition of Mynaric AG, a provider of laser optical communications terminals for air, space, and mobile applications. The total consideration was $155.3 million, comprising a nominal cash component and 2,277,002 shares of Rocket Lab Common Stock. Founder and CEO Peter Beck stated that laser communication is a key enabler for satellite constellations but noted it has been unavailable in adequate volumes. He emphasized Rocket Lab's capability to unlock satellite subsystem bottlenecks and make advanced technology affordable and scalable. Mynaric will remain headquartered in Munich, Germany, establishing Rocket Lab's European presence. The acquisition received approval from Germany's Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Energy. The companies already had an existing relationship through Mynaric supplying optical communication terminals for Rocket Lab's $515 million Space Development Agency contract for 36 satellites and Rocket Lab's $1.3 billion prime contracts. Rocket Lab is a publicly traded company providing launch services, spacecraft, and satellite components for commercial, government, and national security markets.
